subject: chore: post-removal hygiene — stale Playwright/min-h-[60vh] refs in comments
body:
Two leftover stale references from previous sweeps that the
post-removal grep caught:

#1 — daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs header comment (lines 5-25)
  The test file's documentation block referenced "the full
  browser-runtime test lives in dashboard/e2e and runs under
  Playwright in CI" and "JSDOM full app bootstrap... duplicates
  Playwright's E2E coverage." Both Playwright and dashboard/e2e
  were deleted in the previous commit, so the comment was lying
  about the test infrastructure. Rewrote to describe the current
  state: this is the project's only automated layer; browser-runtime
  tests were removed 2026-04-15 with notes in HISTORY.md and TODO.md
  for the future re-introduction plan.

#2 — styles.css drop-zone migration comment (lines 442-446)
  Said `.drop-zone-container → min-h-[60vh] flex flex-col justify-center`
  but Phase 3 of the vanilla-DaisyUI sweep (1e82da2) replaced the
  arbitrary `min-h-[60vh]` with stock `min-h-screen`. Updated the
  comment to match the actual current code.

Both are pure documentation hygiene — no behavior change.

Verified:
  - vite build clean (3.12s)
  - 60/60 tests pass
  - Grep for `playwright|@playwright|dashboard/e2e|test:e2e|test:visual|theme-baselines|browser-runtime`
    in scripts/, dashboard/js/, dashboard/styles.css, dashboard/index.html
    returns zero hits

Tags: chore, hygiene, docs
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: dependency-cleanup
Semver: patch
